title: CTAB genomic DNA extraction from Arabidopsis leaf material
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"igd5","text":"CTAB-based extraction of genomic DNA from Arabidopsis leaf tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3cp8q","text":"Gives reasonable quality and yield of gDNA. Ideal for DNA in PCRs applications, Dye-terminator Sanger sequencing reactions, and cloning. Can be used for next-generation sequencing applications, however, ensure you perform additional cleaning steps and longer centrifuge times. Run on a 1% agarose gel to ensure you have good quality and clean DNA preparation (and Nanodrop), often these preps yield a substantial portion of sheared nucleotides (likely RNA, so make sure you add RNase A during extraction).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"95btr","text":"Ensure you grind your leaf tissue into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or Qiagen tissue lyser (place 1/8\" steel ball bearing into tube with tissue sample).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9iedv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6r6u7","text":"Make sure leaf tissue remains frozen until the addition of CTAB buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Aliquot required volume of CTAB buffer and heat in water bath at 60 ºC for 5-10 minutes immediately before use.
Cool samples to room temperature .
Add 300 µL chloroform and mix thoroughly with a vortex or vigorous shaking for 15 seconds.
Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes at 20,000 rcf.
Add equal volume of ice-cold 2-propanol and mix by inversion.
Centrifuge for 15 min @ 20,000 rcf.
Wash pellet with 1 mL of 70 % ethanol (mix by inversion).
Resuspend gDNA in nuclease-free H 2 O or low EDTA TE buffer (10 mM Tris-Cl pH 8, 0.1 mM EDTA).
Prepare 2% CTAB buffer. A B 1 Reagent [Cf] 2 hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide 2% (w/v) 3 NaCl 1.4 M 4 EDTA (pH 8) 20 mM 5 Tris-Cl (pH 8) 100 mM 2% CTAB buffer recipe
Add 300 µL / 100 mg leaf tissue of CTAB buffer.
Add RNase A solution to a concentration 50-100 µg/mL.
Mix well with a vortex. Invert samples by hand to ensure that all ground tissue is in solution.
Incubate in water bath at 60 ºC for 30 - 60 minutes. Mix tubes periodically by inversion.
Transfer upper aqueous (approx. 200 µL) phase to clean tube.
Repeat steps 9-10 for a cleaner extract.
Incubate for 30-60 min @ -20 °C
Discard supernatant using pipette.
Centrifuge samples @ 9,200 rcf for 5 min.
Remove as much ethanol as possible using a pipette, then allow pellet to air dry for 5 minutes.
Test yield and purity of samples using a Nanodrop and running samples on a 1 % agarose gel.
creator: Diep R R Ganguly
